evals.prompt: Write a technical spec for a new feature that syncs customer profiles between Salesforce and our internal admin tool.
evals.expected_output: A technical-spec-oriented response that frames the audience, goals, constraints, design sections, and rollout or rollback considerations.
assertions: Output identifies the request as a technical specification or equivalent design document lane
Output includes goals or non-goals and at least one constraint or assumption
Output includes rollout, monitoring, rollback, or another delivery-risk section
Output does not default to end-user tutorial framing

evals.prompt: We need a runbook for the payments worker because the on-call team keeps seeing queue backlog spikes during deploys.
evals.expected_output: A runbook-oriented response that includes dependencies, deploy or rollback steps, monitoring, and troubleshooting guidance.
assertions: Output identifies the document as a runbook or operational guide
Output includes monitoring, alerts, or troubleshooting content
Output includes deployment, rollback, or recovery guidance
Output treats the audience as operators or on-call responders

evals.prompt: Document our public REST API for external developers and include request and response examples.
evals.expected_output: A response that recognizes API reference work and routes to API-documentation or explicitly frames the work as OpenAPI or endpoint documentation instead of generic technical writing.
assertions: Output identifies API documentation as a specialized lane
Output mentions api-documentation, OpenAPI, Swagger, or endpoint reference structure
Output does not treat the request as a generic architecture doc or runbook

evals.prompt: Create onboarding help docs for new customers who need screenshots and step-by-step instructions.
evals.expected_output: A response that recognizes end-user help content and routes to user-guide-writing or equivalent tutorial-focused handling.
assertions: Output identifies the audience as end users or customers
Output mentions user-guide-writing, onboarding docs, tutorial structure, or FAQ guidance
Output does not default to an engineering-only document structure
